result.markdown:
--- title: "ex16. Clustering with Regular Kernel (Parameter Links + Covariates)" --- > **Website workflow note.** > This page reflects the current exported clustering API built around `dpmix.cluster()`, `predict()`, `summary()`, and `plot()`. > Last updated: **2026-03-19**. > > For the package-level theory and longer narrative, see the manuscript and the clustering discussion in the main package articles. # Clustering with a Regular Kernel and Covariate-Dependent Parameter Links **Purpose**: Fit a bulk-only clustering model in which covariates affect **component-specific kernel parameters** (`type = "param"`), while mixture weights remain global. --- ## Data Setup ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} data("nc_posX100_p3_k2") dat <- data.frame(y = nc_posX100_p3_k2$y, nc_posX100_p3_k2$X) train_id <- seq_len(70) train_dat <- dat[train_id, , drop = FALSE] test_dat <- dat[-train_id, , drop = FALSE] summary_tbl <- tibble( split = c("train", "test"), n = c(nrow(train_dat), nrow(test_dat)), y_mean = c(mean(train_dat$y), mean(test_dat$y)), y_sd = c(stats::sd(train_dat$y), stats::sd(test_dat$y)), x1_mean = c(mean(train_dat$x1), mean(test_dat$x1)) ) summary_tbl %>% mutate(across(c(y_mean, y_sd, x1_mean), ~ signif(.x, 4))) %>% kable(caption = "Train/test split for the bulk-only clustering workflow", align = "c") %>% kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE, position = "center") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
Train/test split for the bulk-only clustering workflow
split n y_mean y_sd x1_mean
train 70 3.313 2.191 -0.08687
test 30 3.782 2.861 0.24020
````` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} ggplot(train_dat, aes(x = x1, y = y, color = x2)) + geom_point(alpha = 0.65) + labs( title = "Training sample for bulk-only clustering", subtitle = "Covariates drive kernel parameters via type = 'param'", x = "x1", y = "y", color = "x2" ) + theme_minimal() ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex16-clustering-dpm-param_files/figure-html/data-scatter-1.png){width=768} ::: ::: --- ## Fit a Bulk-Only Clustering Model ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} fit_cluster_param <- load_or_fit( "ex16-clustering-dpm-param-fit_cluster_param", dpmix.cluster( y ~ x1 + x2 + x3, data = train_dat, kernel = "normal", type = "param", components = 8, mcmc = mcmc ) ) summary(fit_cluster_param, vars = c("y", "x1", "x2")) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` $K_star [1] 1 $cluster_sizes 1 70 $cluster_profiles cluster n y_mean y_sd x1_mean x1_sd x2_mean x2_sd 1 C1 70 3.312788 2.190948 -0.08687103 0.9558666 -0.04528296 0.5553962 certainty_mean certainty_sd 1 1 0 $certainty Min. 1st Qu. Median Mean 3rd Qu. Max. 1 1 1 1 1 1 $source [1] "train" $burnin [1] 0 $thin [1] 1 attr(,"class") [1] "summary.dpmixgpd_cluster_fit" "list" ``` ::: ::: Here the formula still defines the full covariate structure, but `type = "param"` means the model uses those predictors to shift component-specific kernel parameters rather than the cluster weights. This matches the parameter-link clustering formulation described in the manuscript. --- ## Training Labels and Cluster Summaries ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} cluster_psm_param <- predict(fit_cluster_param, type = "psm") cluster_train_param <- predict(fit_cluster_param, type = "label", return_scores = TRUE) fit_summary_param <- summary( fit_cluster_param, vars = c("y", "x1", "x2"), top_n = 5 ) train_sizes_tbl <- tibble( cluster = paste0("C", names(fit_summary_param$cluster_sizes)), n = as.integer(fit_summary_param$cluster_sizes) ) train_sizes_tbl %>% kable(caption = "Representative training cluster sizes", align = "c") %>% kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE, position = "center") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
Representative training cluster sizes
cluster n
C1 70
````` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} plot(fit_cluster_param, which = "summary", top_n = 5) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex16-clustering-dpm-param_files/figure-html/fit-summary-plot-1.png){width=768} ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} plot(cluster_train_param, type = "sizes", top_n = 5) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex16-clustering-dpm-param_files/figure-html/train-size-plot-1.png){width=768} ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} plot(cluster_psm_param) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex16-clustering-dpm-param_files/figure-html/psm-plot-1.png){width=768} ::: ::: The fit-level and label-level plotting methods expose the same representative partition from different angles: size summaries, response summaries, and the full posterior similarity matrix. --- ## Predict Labels for Held-Out Data ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} cluster_new_param <- predict( fit_cluster_param, newdata = test_dat, type = "label", return_scores = TRUE ) new_summary_param <- summary( cluster_new_param, vars = c("y", "x1", "x2"), top_n = 5 ) new_sizes_tbl <- tibble( cluster = paste0("C", names(new_summary_param$cluster_sizes)), n = as.integer(new_summary_param$cluster_sizes) ) new_sizes_tbl %>% kable(caption = "Predicted cluster sizes for the held-out sample", align = "c") %>% kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE, position = "center") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
Predicted cluster sizes for the held-out sample
cluster n
C1 30
````` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} plot(cluster_new_param, type = "summary", top_n = 5) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex16-clustering-dpm-param_files/figure-html/new-summary-plot-1.png){width=768} ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} plot(cluster_new_param, type = "certainty") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](ex16-clustering-dpm-param_files/figure-html/new-certainty-plot-1.png){width=768} ::: ::: For newdata, the labels are returned in the space of the representative training clusters, which makes train/test comparison straightforward without exposing component-label switching from the sampler. --- ## Takeaways - `dpmix.cluster()` is the current wrapper for **bulk-only** clustering. - `type = "param"` is appropriate when covariates should alter **within-cluster regression/parameter structure** while keeping global mixture weights. - The same three-step workflow applies as in the spliced example: fit, `predict(..., type = "psm" | "label")`, then `summary()` and `plot()`. - This closes the examples sequence with the two main clustering modes now exposed by the package website. --- ## Workflow Navigation - Previous: [ex15-clustering-dpmgpd-weights](ex15-clustering-dpmgpd-weights.html) - Next: [troubleshooting](../start/troubleshooting.html) - Workflow index: [Roadmap](../start/roadmap.html) - Practical entry: [Examples](../examples/index.html)
